a: A check for language support and status.
The availability status for a language or language pairing.
The framework supports the language or language pairing and has it downloaded and ready for use in a translation.
The framework supports the language or language pairing, but can’t yet use it.
The framework doesn’t support the language or language pairing.
Display simple system translations and create custom translation experiences.
Translate text in your app from one language to another.
Error codes describing why the framework can’t perform a translation.
A class that performs translations between a pair of languages.
A type that provides asynchronous access to translation responses.
The type of asynchronous iterator that produces elements of this asynchronous sequence.
The type of element produced by this asynchronous sequence.
A type containing the information to use when performing a translation.
A translation request containing a single item of text to translate.
The response to a translation request.
The preferred model to handle translations in your app.
An error describing a translation session that you’ve cancelled before, which prevents the session from producing additional results.
The text to translate, including styling like bold text, italics, and hyperlink data for linking between data sources.
The original translated text, including style formatting and links.
The translated formatted text.
A boolean value that indicates whether a translation session can request language downloads.
Attempts to stop all ongoing work for the translation session.
An optional unique identifier to associate a translation request with its response.
The unique identifier matching the client identifier set in the translation request.
A localized message describing the error.
A localized message describing the reason for the failure.
A translation strategy that provides more fluent translations using Apple Intelligence.
Creates an instance to check what languages are available.
Creates a translation session to translate between a given source and target language already installed on device.
Creates a translation session to translate between a given source and target language already installed on device.
Creates an instance for checking language availability with a preferred translation strategy.
Creates a configuration from a source and target language.
Creates a configuration from a source and target language.
Creates an instance of a translation response with formatted text.
Creates an instance of a translation response.
Creates a request for translating a single attributed string.
Creates a request for translating a single string of text.
An error occurred internal to the translation engine.
Invalidate the current translation session and re-run it with new content.
A boolean value that indicates whether the system has installed the source and target languages of the session and is ready to begin translation.
A translation strategy that provides fast translations using traditional models.
Creates the asynchronous iterator that produces elements of this asynchronous sequence.
The device doesn’t have the necessary languages downloaded to perform a translation and the session can’t request the person to download them.
No content to translate.
The preferred strategy of translation to check availability for.
The preferred translation strategy configured for the session.
The translation approach for this configuration.
Asks for permission to download translation languages without doing any translations.
The language to translate content from.
The input language to translate from.
The language that the framework translated the text from.
The plain text input to translate.
The original text to translate from.
Checks to see if the framework supports the language pairing based off a string of sample text.
Checks for the installation of a specific language pairing and whether it’s ready for translation.
A list of translation languages the framework supports.
The language to translate content into.
The output language to translate into.
The language that the framework translated the text into.
The translated text.
Translates a formatted string of text, preserving formatting in the translation.
Translates a single string of text.
Translates multiple strings of text of the same language, returning a sequence of responses as they’re available.
Translates multiple strings of text of the same language, returning the results all at once when complete.
The framework can’t identify the source language automatically.
The framework doesn’t support the specified source and target language pairing.
The framework doesn’t support the specified or detected source language.
The framework doesn’t support the specified or chosen target language.
A value the equals function uses to represent change in the configuration instance.
This operator allows you to check for a given value of a translation error and handle each error case.
